zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabell De Toft 14GGM於1450年出生在英國艾可蕾托 巴多福時，她的父親Sir William Toft25歲，母親Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM26歲。她於1465年與Thomas Smith在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1510年在英國艾可享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabell De Toft 14GGM于1450年出生在英国艾可蕾托 巴多福时，她的父亲Sir William Toft25岁，母亲Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM26岁。她于1465年与Thomas Smith在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1510年在英国艾可享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toft
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Hjalmer, Nels, Thorval.

English (Lancashire, Cheshire and Staffordshire): habitational name from any of various places, for example in Cambridgeshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, and Warwickshire, named with Middle English toft(e) (Old Norse topt, Old Danish toft), denoting a plot of land on which a house stands or formerly stood. The surname may also be topographic, for someone who lived by or on a toft.
Scandinavian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads or placenames derived from Old Norse topt ‘curtilage, site, homestead’.

variants.name: Tufts
meanings: English (Norfolk): variant of Tofts, itself a variant of Toft with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Tift
meanings: English (Midlands): variant of   Toft  .

variants.name: Tuft
meanings: English (Staffordshire): variant of   Toft  .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farmsteads so named, from Old Norse tupt ‘site, lot’.
Possibly an altered form of South German Duft, a topographic name meaning ‘swamp, moor’.

variants.name: Taft
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire and Derbyshire): variant of   Toft  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial or metonymic occupational name from German Taft ‘taffeta’.

History: Robert Taft (born c. 1640), lived in Braintree, MA, and subsequently Mendon, MA. — Alphonso Taft (1810–91), jurist and politician born in Townshend, VT, was the father of William Howard Taft (1857–1930), 27th president of the US and chief justice of the US Supreme Court.

variants.name: Tofte
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Hjalmer, Iver, Steinar, Thora.
Scandinavian (mainly Norwegian): variant of   Toft  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabell De Toft 14GGM was born in 1450, in Little Baddow, Essex, England, her father, Sir William Toft, was 25 and her mother, Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM, was 26. She married Thomas Smith in 1465, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1510, in Essex, England, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabell De Toft 14GGM est née en 1450, à Little Baddow, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Toft, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Smith en 1465, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1510, à Essex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabell De Toft 14GGM 1450 in Little Baddow, Essex, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Toft 25 und ihre Mutter Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM 26. Sie hat 1465 in Essex, England Thomas Smith geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1510 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Essex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell De Toft 14GGM è nata il nel 1450 nel Little Baddow, Essex, England, sua padre, Sir William Toft, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Smith nel 1465, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1510, nel Essex, England, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabell De Toft 14GGMが1450年にイギリスエセックスリットル バッドオウで生まれた時，父Sir William Toftは25歳，母Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGMは26歳であった。1465年にイギリスエセックスでThomas Smithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1510年にイギリスエセックスで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabell De Toft 14GGM(이)가 1450년 영국 에세스 리틀 바도에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Toft(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1465년 영국 에세스에서 Thomas Smith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 영국 에세스에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabell De Toft 14GGM nasceu em 1450, em Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Toft, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM, tinha 26 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabell De Toft 14GGM родилась в 1450 г. в местности литл баддоу, эссекс, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Toft было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Smith в 1465 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1510 г. в местности эссекс, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabell De Toft 14GGM nació en 1450, en Little Baddow, Essex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Toft, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Margaret Leventhorpe 15GGM, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Thomas Smith en 1465, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1510, en Essex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 60 años.
